codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 6 points: (1) AC3/AC4 cmd1 grep pattern not set -e safe and treats grep exit=2 (file-not-found) as passing sentinel — PROPOSED_FIX provided; (2) variable named 'flock' in ordering check does not shadow binary — OBSERVATION_ONLY; (3) single-quoted LOCK_TIMEOUT grep patterns are shell-safe — OBSERVATION_ONLY; (4) multi-line decl input causes [ -lt ] to error-exit (confirmed not a false-positive path); (5) backslash-dollar BRE matches literal dollar correctly — OBSERVATION_ONLY; (6) score-update.sh exclusion is correct given spec says 'optional' — OBSERVATION_ONLY. Codex recommended FAIL on AC3/AC4 shell-safety.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Finding 1 evaluated: grep exit=2 false-positive is a real theoretical weakness but non-blocking because (a) AC1 gates file existence first — if the file doesn't exist, AC1 fails before AC3/AC4 run; (b) the test harness runs commands in plain bash, not set -e context; (c) the failure mode only occurs if the target files do not exist at all. The concern is recorded as a codex_observation_only note but does not block verdict. All other findings classified as OBSERVATION_ONLY and do not affect verdict.

codex_observation_only.observation: AC3 cmd1 and AC4 cmd1 use the pattern 'grep ...; [ $? -ne 0 ] && echo SENTINEL'. If grep exits 2 (file not found / unreadable), [ $? -ne 0 ] evaluates true and emits the sentinel incorrectly, creating a false-pass. Under a set -e shell, grep's exit=1 (no matches) would abort the compound command before [ $? -ne 0 ] is reached. Codex proposed fix: use 'if grep ...; then false; else rc=$?; [ $rc -eq 1 ] && echo SENTINEL || exit $rc; fi'. This is a hardening improvement for future AC authoring but does not block this cycle because AC1 (bash -n) gates file existence — the grep exit=2 path cannot occur after AC1 passes.

AC2 ordering check uses variable named 'flock' (e.g., flock=$(grep -n ...)). Codex confirmed this does NOT shadow the flock binary — shell variables and command names are separate namespaces. Renaming to 'flock_line' would improve clarity but is not required for correctness.
Single-quoted grep patterns for LOCK_TIMEOUT string are shell-safe. Locale is not a risk for this literal ASCII pattern. Using grep -F would be clearer but current BRE form is correct.

AC3 cmd2 pattern 'grep -c "flock -s -w "\${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 8"' — backslash-dollar in BRE matches literal dollar in the file. The file does not need to contain a literal backslash. Confirmed correct. Using grep -F would be clearer.

dimension_results.rationale: scripts/score-inject.sh and scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh are the only files in scope. scripts/score-update.sh is explicitly named in Won't Have: 'Do NOT modify scripts/score-update.sh (false positive; out of scope)'. Spec Section 7 Step 3 says score-update.sh fix is 'optional' and 'RECOMMENDED but NOT required'. Spec Section 8(b) instructs to 'minimize diff surface'. BA's conservative Won't Have decision aligns with spec guidance and prevents new introduced_in_diff violations. No scope_alignment objection raised.

BA directly Read both target scripts and confirmed exact line content: score-inject.sh:64 has 'if ! flock -s -w 10 8; then' and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh:52 has 'if ! flock -w 10 9; then'. Adjacent stderr messages at lines 65 and 53 confirmed to contain 'within 10s'. Root cause is confirmed by direct file evidence, not just inferred from spec description. BA codex_consult.invoked: true with 7 findings, all applied. Codex also independently ran experiments to verify AC command behavior. Evidence quality is high for a MICRO fix.

The requirement is a MICRO static fix (no runtime investigation needed). BA scoped via grep search pattern 'flock.*-w 10' over scripts/, found 3 occurrences, correctly classified 2 as genuine violations and 1 (score-update.sh:76) as false positive per provision alpha. Codex consultation raised and resolved 7 findings. No investigation deliverables were required by the spec beyond identifying the exact lines — BA provided them.

Both affected files verified to exist and contain exactly the content BA claims: scripts/score-inject.sh line 64 'if ! flock -s -w 10 8; then', line 65 'within 10s'; scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh line 52 'if ! flock -w 10 9; then', line 53 'within 10s'. BA's component_chain line_range references (61-67 and 48-55) are consistent with the actual file structure. files_to_modify list contains exactly the two in-scope files.

AC commands were run verbatim in bash to verify correctness. AC1 command 'bash -n scripts/score-inject.sh && bash -n scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh' matches the spec's syntax-check requirement exactly. AC2 grep patterns use single-quoted 'LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"' which correctly matches the literal string the spec requires. AC3 patterns check for the exact forms 'flock -s -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 8' and 'flock -w "${LOCK_TIMEOUT}" 9' as specified in the spec. AC4 patterns check 'within ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s' absence and presence as required. No spec-text-vs-execution drift detected — all commands faithfully represent the spec's verifiable requirements. The Codex-flagged grep exit-code-2 concern is a hardening gap, not a spec drift.

success_criteria_verification.criterion: bash -n syntax check exits 0 for both scripts
LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}" declaration present before flock call in both scripts
commands_verified: decl=$(grep -n 'LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"' scripts/score-inject.sh | cut -d: -f1); flock=$(grep -n 'flock.*LOCK_TIMEOUT' scripts/score-inject.sh | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); [ -n "$decl" ] && [ -n "$flock" ] && [ "$decl" -lt "$flock" ] && echo OK

decl=$(grep -n 'LOCK_TIMEOUT="${LOCK_TIMEOUT:-10}"' scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh | cut -d: -f1); flock=$(grep -n 'flock.*LOCK_TIMEOUT' scripts/lifecycle-baseline-import.sh | head -1 | cut -d: -f1); [ -n "$decl" ] && [ -n "$flock" ] && [ "$decl" -lt "$flock" ] && echo OK
success_criteria_verification.notes: Single-quoted patterns prevent shell expansion; $() nesting with inner single quotes tested in bash -c context and confirmed working. Ordering check uses numeric line comparison. Multi-line decl input causes integer-expression error and fails (not a false positive — Codex confirmed). Each file verified independently.

success_criteria_verification.criterion: No bare flock -s -w 10 or flock -w 10 remaining in either script
success_criteria_verification.notes: Cmd1 exit-code-2 false-positive concern noted (codex_observation_only) but non-blocking: AC1 gates file existence. BRE backslash-dollar matches literal dollar correctly (confirmed). Each file verified independently for presence checks.

success_criteria_verification.criterion: Adjacent stderr messages use ${LOCK_TIMEOUT}s not hardcoded 10s
success_criteria_verification.notes: Same pattern as AC3 cmd1. Exit-code-2 concern same as AC3 (non-blocking). Per-file presence checks are independent.

implementation_note_verification.location_in_ticket: Must Have section and Implementation Note section both specify these requirements

out_of_scope_observations.observation: flock -w 10 — same style debt as the genuine violations in score-inject.sh and lifecycle-baseline-import.sh. Spec Section 7 Step 3 recommends (but does not require) applying the same parameterization. BA chose Won't Have to minimize diff surface per Section 8(b). Recommend addressing in a separate cleanup cycle after close.
